title: Advanced label conventions: defaults, aliases, reference validation, hard rejection
description:
Follow-ups deferred from ba97487f (label constraint config). Each sub-bullet is its own eventual task; this story tracks them together so they don't get lost.

Deferred items:
- Default value auto-apply: NamespaceConfig.default: String. On issue create, if no label from that namespace is present, auto-apply the default. Generalizes existing validation.default_type.
- Deprecation/alias rewriting: map like priority:p1 -> priority:high. jit label migrate rewrites in bulk; add/validate respect the alias on read/write.
- Reference validation: values of form <slug> can be required to resolve to an existing issue (e.g. epic:<slug> must match an issue with type:epic and that slug).
- Hard rejection at add-label time: when enforce_namespace_registry=true and values/pattern are set, reject in add_label() not only in validate.
- max cardinality on NamespaceConfig (unique becomes sugar for max=1).
- Built-in labels-valid gate so repos can wire label correctness into the gate system rather than relying only on CLI-time rejection.
- Retroactive strict defaults for existing repos (needs migration story; intentionally skipped in ba97487f which only touches new-repo init).

Depends on ba97487f (config schema + validate surfacing must exist first).
